adventure.explanation: Riders tackle a 122K circuit through Paseo del Bajo at Km 55.2 and AU Perito Moreno feed zone at Km 35, pedaling on highways closed to traffic.[1][2]

altitude.explanation: The urban circuit stays at near-sea level with no significant climbs across 122K in flat Buenos Aires terrain.[1][2]

climate.overview: Mild spring conditions ideal for cycling.
climate.wind_conditions: Moderate coastal breezes possible; flat terrain exposes groups to crosswinds.
climate.explanation: October's spring weather in Buenos Aires delivers mild 20-25°C temperatures ideal for the 6:00 am start at Parque Sarmiento.[2][3]

community.explanation: Hundreds of riders join mass starts by category at Av. Balbín 4750, followed by a Brunch Party for all at Tercer Tiempo in Parque Sarmiento.[1][2]

elevation.explanation: Total elevation remains under 100m on the flat 122K loop weaving through Av. 9 de Julio and Pampa y Lugones.[2]

expenses.explanation: Foreigners pay U$S175 for the 122K entry, unlocking aid stations with fresh fruit and the post-race brunch at Parque Sarmiento.[1][2]

experience.explanation: Timing chips track partial times at four checkpoints like PC3 on Av. 9 de Julio at Km 92, with medical aid across the closed Buenos Aires streets.[1][2]

field_depth.explanation: Categories host mass starts from corrals at Av. Balbín 4750 in Parque Sarmiento, drawing hundreds of riders of varying fitness levels.[1][2]

length.explanation: The long distance covers 122K with cut-offs like 10:20AM at Km 92 on AU 25 de Mayo from Av. 9 de Julio sur.[2]

logistics.overview: Check-in at Bike + Mobility Expo in Parque Sarmiento on Friday Oct 10 and Saturday Oct 11, 2025; race start Sunday Oct 12 at Av. Rosario V. Peñaloza & Av. Calabria from 6 AM.
logistics.accommodation: Abundant hotels in Buenos Aires; stay near Palermo or Centro for easy access to Parque Sarmiento.
logistics.getting_there: Fly into Buenos Aires Ezeiza (EZE) or Aeroparque (AEP); use taxis, buses, or bikes to Parque Sarmiento.
logistics.parking: Limited; event promotes sustainable mobility—use public transport or bike to expo.
logistics.spectator_info: Free viewing along closed circuit; best at start/finish in Parque Sarmiento and expo area.
logistics.explanation: Start at 6:00 am from Av. Balbín 4750 with broom vehicles for cut-off misses at PC4 Peaje Avellaneda Km 103, fully closed to traffic.[1][2]

race_quality.explanation: Aid stations at Km 12 Av. Pampa and Av. Lugones offer cereals and hydration, plus finisher medals right after crossing at Av. Balbín 4750.[1][2]

technicality.explanation: Flat streets demand low skills on the 122K circuit with feed zones requiring one foot down at AU Perito Moreno Km 35.[2]

value.explanation: U$S175 grants 122K on closed highways past Paseo del Bajo, finisher medal, and brunch party at Parque Sarmiento for all finishers.[1][2]

cultural_impact.explanation: Promotes cycling mobility, health, and inclusion in Buenos Aires.

prestige.explanation: This third edition on October 11, 2026, lacks UCI status unlike Granfondo Siete Lagos, but earns City sporting interest declaration.[3][4]
